w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring four NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 680GB RAM for high-performance AI model training and large-scale machine learning workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 680GB RAM for intensive AI/ML workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring 4 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 680GB of RAM, designed for high-performance AI and machine learning model training.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server built on an x86_64 architecture utilizing 48 vCPUs across 24 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.2 GHz. The system provides 680 GB of RAM and includes four NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs, delivering a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via 1500 GB of NVMe SSD. Benchmark performance indicates average multi-core CPU and memory throughput, while LLM inference tasks demonstrate top-tier performance, particularly in prompt processing and large model text generation. The architecture supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets for specialized computation. With a dedicated CPU allocation and significant GPU resources, this configuration is engineered for demanding machine learning model training, complex AI inference, and large-scale data science jobs requiring substantial parallel processing capabilities and high-bandwidth memory access.
